headline: Uber expands Women Preferences, Hims & Hers stock skyrockets
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma and Market Domination host Josh Lipton discuss some of Monday's top trending stories. Ticketmaster parent company Live Nation (LYV) reached a settlement in a monopolization lawsuit with the US Department of Justice (DOJ). Hims & Hers (HIMS) stock skyrocketed on Monday after a big win: the company struck a deal with Novo Nordisk (NVO) to bring GLP-1 drugs Ozempic and Wegovy onto its platform. Uber's (UBER) "Women Preferences" feature is expanding nationwide. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: Lucid Announces Agenda Ahead of Investor Day on March 12
summary: Lucid Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: LCID), maker of the world's most advanced electric vehicles, today announced the agenda for its upcoming Investor Day. The event will be webcast beginning at 8:00 a.m. ET on March 12, 2026, and will offer the investment community and media a detailed view of Lucid's strategic priorities, key growth drivers, and path to sustainable value creation and improved financial performance.

headline: Uber Expands Women Driver Matching Nationwide Amid Ongoing US Legal Cases
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) is expanding a safety-focused ride option nationwide in the US, rolling out a feature that allows female riders to request women drivers as the company continues navigating legal scrutiny tied to passenger assault claims.  The option, labeled Women Drivers, will appear alongside existing categories such as UberX, Comfort, UberXL and Black, with availability extending to major markets including New York, Philadelphia and Washington, DC after earlier pilots and launches across more than two dozen US cities last year.  Uber has already introduced similar matching options in several international markets, including Germany and France, and the feature is available to drivers in more than 40 countries.

headline: Uber Women Preferences Rollout Tests Safety Focus And Legal Risk
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) is rolling out its "Women Preferences" feature to riders and drivers across the US. The tool allows women and non-binary drivers to indicate a preference for picking up women riders, and women riders to request women drivers. The expansion comes as Uber faces a class action lawsuit that alleges the feature is discriminatory. For you as an investor, this update sits at the intersection of product design, user safety, and regulation for a large global ridesharing...

headline: Nissan, Uber near deal to deploy autonomous ride-hailing vehicles
summary: The Japanese automaker is partnering with UK-based startup Wayve Technologies - backed by Uber - to develop self-driving systems for future ride-hailing fleets.

headline: Uber's women-only option goes nationwide in the US
summary: Uber launched a feature Monday to allow both women riders and drivers across the U.S. to be matched with other women for trips, expanding a pilot program aimed at addressing concerns about the safety of its riding-hailing platform.  The new feature is being rolled out nationwide despite an ongoing class action lawsuit against the policy in California, filed by Uber drivers who argue that it is discriminatory against men.  The feature, announced in a blog post, allows women to request a female driver through an option on the app called “Women Drivers.”

The new feature is being rolled out nationwide despite an ongoing class action lawsuit against the policy in California, filed by Uber drivers who argue that it is discriminatory against men.  The feature, announced in a blog post, allows women to request a female driver through an option on the app called “Women Drivers.”

headline: Is UBER's Cheap Valuation Reason Enough to Invest in the Stock?
summary: Uber trades at a steep discount to industrial levels. Despite strong gross bookings growth and expansion moves, robotaxi competition and regulatory risks cloud the near-term outlook.
